module_mood_contract: earned trust opening — first curated-skill invocation as subagent (M3 Ex1)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1=8, Phase 2=8, Phase 3=8 (all teaching beats clear floor). Phase 4 = setup/handoff, mood-exempt per carve-out (compresses to parenthetical).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M3 Ex1 (line 326): 'Invoke the curated access-control analysis skill on the feature as a subagent... Name the delta — this is the access-mapping that STRIDE will consume.' File body delivers exactly this; hands off to STRIDE in 'What this sets up' (line 76).
Body framing is 'curated skill does breadth; you own codebase-specific judgment.' Earned-trust register, not failure-shame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Forcing moves (invoke skill, walk the map, add the delta) live in prompts map-the-access-surface-1 through -5. Body line 62 ('Push back on the sharpening question until the reason names something specific to your codebase') is the engineer-audience body callout per §56 — recoverable in <1min, codebase-specific contrast example grounds it. Maintainer P3 push-back covers the failure mode.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches work, no authored conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with neighbor', 'tell the room', or pair-discussion authoring in body. Line 68 ('Most people skip this. Some want the pause. Your call.') is student-agency framing per §10, not cohort management.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 107-110: three falsifiable outcomes (invokes ACA as subagent before review; writes delta note with both shapes codebase-specific; adds at least one codebase-specific surface).
Maintainer Push-back moves (lines 91-96) cover P1 blocker, P1 skill-invocation, P2 skip, P3 generic, P3 zero misses, P4 rush — each with named failure + Nerd recovery line.
Plug points (lines 103-105) name student's feature + ACA skill with install location. Skill install referenced in body (line 14). Window callout (line 5) routes session.
Line 5: '**Window:** *m3-security* (the main lane from *Open the side quest*; m3-quality stays parked).' One-line lane callout, doesn't disturb teaching content.

Body line 9 names the principle: 'STRIDE without an access-surface map is pub-quiz threat modeling. Before you threat-model, you map what you're protecting.' Mechanism (skill does breadth, student owns codebase-specific judgment) is explicit.

Exercise file; Key Concepts section lives in module spine, not exercise. AE101 M3 themes (mirror, 90% correct, mechanically self-aware) instance through the delta move (skill's read mirrored against student's read).
No lecture in this exercise file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview prompt payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'What you do' (line 7) names activity sequence; 'The point' (line 9) names the discipline. Neither previews what the prompts will REVEAL. 'What happened' (line 72) sits POST-action as recap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 62 push-back guidance is body-callout shape; engineer can notice generic-delta miss in <1min via maintainer P3 push-back. No silent miss carries downstream.
